title: Set-Content without -Encoding parameter produces system-default encoding
description: Set-Content without an explicit -Encoding parameter uses the system default encoding (Windows-1252 on PS 5.1, UTF-8 NoBOM on PS 7). This creates inconsistent file encoding across PS versions and can introduce the UTF-8 BOM issues tracked in SIN-PATTERN-006. JSON, XML, and XHTML files are particularly vulnerable.
detection_method: Regex scan for Set-Content calls without -Encoding parameter
preventionRule: All Set-Content calls must include -Encoding UTF8 (or UTF8BOM where PS 5.1 parsing is involved). Out-File should also specify -Encoding.
remedy: Add -Encoding UTF8 parameter to the Set-Content call. For PS 5.1 script files, use -Encoding UTF8 (which adds BOM in PS 5.1).
